pages.raw_text:
The office of inspector of weights
and measures was created by the city
council last night The incumbent
is appointed by the mayor, Is remov-
able at will and is paid entirely by
fees.


The ordinance requires an inspec-
ton every year of all weights. meas
ures and scales except track scales
and those weighing three tons or up
ward, which must be inspected every
six months. The fees range from
$3.50 for track scales to cents for
different measures.


An ordinance for a steel viaduct
over Washington street between First
and Second street was passed. A
contract with the Washington Water
Power company for street sprinkling
on certain streets by electric sprlnk-
ling cars was authorized.


A petition from business men ask
ing for an increase In salaries of
policemen was received. An ordinance
increasing the price Of day labor on
city work from 81 to $2.25 was
passed.


An ordinance providing for the pur-
chase of a Decarie crematory plant.
costing $22.500. was introduced by
Councilman Johnson.
